text: just found a $47K website redesign contract on SAM.gov. small business set-aside. zero large company competition. applying today. the government literally gives small businesses preferential access and most people have no idea.
the US government awarded $183 billion in contracts to small businesses last year. billion with a B. 23% of all federal spending. and 90% of solopreneurs don't even know SAM.gov exists.

registered on SAM.gov two weeks ago. already found 22 IT contracts under $150K with deadlines this month. small business set-asides mean i'm only competing against other small shops. not Lockheed Martin.
the micro-purchase threshold is $10K. that means government agencies can buy services under $10K without formal bidding. they just pick someone from SAM.gov and send a PO. be that someone.

found an Air Force RFI for SBOM vulnerability scanning tools. NAICS 541519. they're looking for cybersecurity solutions and specifically want small businesses to respond. this is a $0 cost lead gen pipeline.

gov contracting hack: search SAM.gov for 'sources sought' notices. these are the government literally asking if anyone can do the job BEFORE they post the real contract. respond to every one in your lane.

someone asked me why i'm pursuing gov contracts as a solopreneur. the simplified acquisition threshold is $350K. under that they use streamlined procedures that FAVOR small businesses. the game is rigged in our favor and we're not playing.
day 3 of gov contract hunting. found a VA hospital looking for IT services. small business set-aside. response deadline in 2 weeks. drafted my capability statement in 45 minutes. submitting tomorrow.
the NAICS codes that print for solopreneurs in gov contracting: 541511 (custom programming), 541512 (computer systems design), 541519 (other IT services), 518210 (cloud/hosting). memorize these.
8(a) certification lets you get sole-source contracts up to $4.5M without competition. SDVOSB gets you set-asides at the VA worth billions annually. certifications are cheat codes in this space.

spent 2 hours on SAM.gov this morning. filtered by: NAICS 541512 + small business set-aside + response deadline next 30 days. got 47 results. 12 are IT modernization projects under $250K. this is a gold mine nobody talks about.

cold emailed a government contractor who won a $2.3M web development contract last year. asked if they need subcontractors. they said yes. my first gov contract revenue might be as a sub, not a prime. doesn't matter. get in the door.

wild stat: the government simplified acquisition threshold went from $250K to $350K. that means MORE contracts use streamlined small-business-friendly procedures now. the window got bigger and nobody noticed.

built a SAM.gov monitoring script. runs every morning at 6 AM. filters for my NAICS codes + small business set-asides + deadlines in 30 days. emails me a summary. took 3 hours to build. will save thousands in opportunity cost.

just submitted my first capability statement to a government agency. 2 pages. past performance, NAICS codes, certifications, key personnel. took 90 minutes. this one document could unlock millions in contract revenue over the next 5 years.
gov contract opportunity: Department of Commerce needs patent technical services. $114.4M contract. small businesses can sub. even getting 2% of that as a subcontractor is $2.2M. stop thinking small.

the government's fiscal year ends September 30. agencies MUST spend their remaining budget or lose it. August-September is 'use it or lose it' season. that's when micro-purchases and quick contracts spike. mark your calendar now.

most people look at a $50M government contract and think 'that's not for me.' they're right about the prime. but every $50M prime needs 5-15 subcontractors. that's where solopreneurs fit. find the prime, pitch the sub.

HUBZone certification hack: if your home office is in a HUBZone (check SBA's map), you qualify for price evaluation preferences and sole-source contracts up to $7M. i just checked. my zip code qualifies.

day 7 of gov contract journey. 3 capability statements sent. 1 sources sought response submitted. 2 subcontractor introductions made. zero revenue yet but building the pipeline. this is a 90-day game minimum.

USAspending.gov shows every dollar the government spends. i searched my NAICS code. found 240 awards last year totaling $118M. now i know exactly which agencies buy what i sell and which contractors win.

gov contracting pro tip: attend industry days. agencies host free events where they tell you exactly what they're buying next quarter. bring business cards. meet the contracting officers. most small businesses skip these.

the Army just posted a sources sought for commercial mass text messaging services at Fort Hood. small business set-aside. they need a subscription text platform. this is literally a SaaS resale opportunity for <$50K.

GSA Schedule (now MAS) lets you be a pre-approved government vendor. once you're on schedule, agencies can buy from you without a new competition every time. it's a moat. application takes 4-6 months but worth it.

week 2 update: found 67 relevant contracts on SAM.gov. responded to 4 sources sought notices. connected with 2 prime contractors who need subs. still $0 revenue but the funnel is building. this is how you break into a $750B market.

just learned about GovWin and Bloomberg Government. they track upcoming contracts BEFORE they hit SAM.gov. that's the real edge. knowing about a $5M IT contract 6 months before everyone else sees it. paying for intel pays.

government agencies are required to give 23% of contracts to small businesses. they get graded on this. some agencies are BEHIND on their goals. those are the ones most likely to award to you. check SBA scorecard data.

my SAM.gov registration just went active. took 12 business days. got my CAGE code and UEI number. now i can officially respond to federal solicitations. one registration, access to $750B in annual spending.

broke down the easiest gov contracts for solopreneurs: 1. website development 2. social media management 3. content writing 4. IT support 5. training/workshops 6. data analysis 7. cybersecurity assessments. all under $150K, all small biz set-asides.

WOSB (women-owned small business) and EDWOSB get sole-source contracts up to $7M for manufacturing and $4.5M for services. if you qualify or can partner with someone who does, that's another lane with less competition.

the government posted 22 new IT opportunities on SAM.gov today. i set up email alerts for my NAICS codes. takes 30 seconds. new contracts land in my inbox before most contractors check the website. speed is edge.

found a Department of Interior contract for appraisal services. small business set-aside. deadline Feb 17. the scope: appraise one tract of land in Liberty County TX. probably worth $15-25K. these small contracts are where you start.

gov contract math: even at a 5% win rate, if you respond to 20 opportunities per quarter averaging $75K each, that's $75K per quarter from one win. scale to 40 responses and it's $150K. volume is the strategy.

unpopular take: gov contracting is better than cold email for solopreneurs. cold email: 3% reply rate, unknown budget, price shopping. gov contracts: budget is public, requirements are clear, payment is guaranteed by the US Treasury.

the Forest Service just posted a trail hardening construction project in Texas. small business set-aside. design-build. these aren't just for tech. if you know someone in construction, forward them this. network = net worth.

3 free tools for gov contract hunting that most people don't know: 1. SAM.gov alerts (email when new contracts match your NAICS) 2. USAspending.gov (see who won what) 3. FPDS.gov (detailed award data). all free. zero excuses.

just learned the government must post contracts for 15 days minimum before closing. that means you have at minimum 2 weeks to respond to anything you find. stop panicking about deadlines and start submitting.

gov contracting month 1 recap: SAM.gov registered. 4 NAICS codes identified. 67 opportunities tracked. 4 responses submitted. 2 subcontractor intros made. monitoring script built. the pipeline exists where there was nothing 30 days ago.

someone told me 'government contracts are only for big companies.' the SBA literally exists to prove that wrong. 23% mandated for small business. $183B awarded last year. these people are not paying attention.

the VA spent $37B on small business contracts last year. they're required to give 15% to service-disabled veteran-owned businesses. if you're a veteran or know one who wants to start a business, this is the lane.

gov contract hack nobody talks about: find contracts that got zero bids. they get re-posted with lower requirements or extended deadlines. less competition on round 2. search SAM.gov for amendments and extensions.

built a template for responding to sources sought notices. 3 pages max. company overview, relevant experience, technical approach, contact info. reusable for every response. template it once, spam it forever.

the government buys EVERYTHING. websites, apps, training, janitorial, food, consulting, IT, construction, medical devices, furniture, marketing. whatever you do, there's a NAICS code for it and someone paying for it.

day 14. got my first response from a government contracting officer. they want to discuss my capability statement. it's not a contract yet but it's a conversation with someone who controls a $2M annual IT budget. conversations lead to contracts.

the simplified acquisition threshold going to $350K is massive. under $350K the process is faster, less paperwork, and agencies have more flexibility to pick small businesses. this is your sweet spot as a solopreneur.

federal IT spending is projected to grow 1-2% in FY2026. that's still $100B+. while VCs are tightening, the government is INCREASING IT spend. most stable customer in the world. they literally print the money they pay you with.

month 1 submitted: 4 sources sought responses, 2 capability statements to primes, 1 simplified acquisition bid. month 2 plan: 10 responses, 5 prime contractor meetings, attend 2 industry days. scaling the pipeline.

your capability statement is your resume for government contracting. mine includes: UEI number, CAGE code, NAICS codes, past performance, differentiators, contact info. 2 pages. designed in Canva. took 2 hours. uses it for everything now.

reminder: the government MUST pay invoices within 30 days (Prompt Payment Act). late payments accrue interest automatically. try getting that from a private client. 'net 30' that actually means net 30. what a concept.

mapped my gov contract strategy for Q2: 1. get GSA Schedule application started 2. respond to 15 solicitations 3. attend 3 industry days 4. partner with 2 established primes as sub 5. target first contract win by June. aggressive but doable.
# INCOME DISCLAIMER: Results not typical. Individual results vary based on effort
